goal: slop.language.ast: decompose flat Node enum into 9 semantic per-category enums (Identifier, Callable, Scope, Wrapper, Conditional, Loop, Switch, Catch, Operator), one per module

summary.title: Per-category enum split for slop.language.ast
summary.what: Replaced the flat Node StrEnum with nine semantic per-category enums, one per module: Identifier, Callable, Scope, Wrapper, Conditional, Loop, Switch, Catch, Operator. Each is a StrEnum; behavior identical to the flat Node (members are str-equivalent). Updated all 11 grammar files to import only the categories they reference. The single flat nodes.py removed. 486 tests pass; zero behavior change.
summary.why: The flat Node enum (~100 members) was a single source of truth but lacked semantic structure — a reader scanning a grammar file saw 'Node.IF_STATEMENT' next to 'Node.FUNCTION_DEFINITION' next to 'Node.BINARY_EXPRESSION' with no shape signaling category. Splitting into per-category enums makes the grammar files self-documenting: 'Conditional.IF_STATEMENT' next to 'Loop.FOR_STATEMENT' tells the reader what's going on without reading the docstring.
summary.user_goal: slop.language.ast: decompose flat Node enum into 9 semantic per-category enums (Identifier, Callable, Scope, Wrapper, Conditional, Loop, Switch, Catch, Operator), one per module

decisions.chose: Catch (not Exception) for the try/catch/rescue family.
decisions.rationale: Shadowing Python's built-in Exception class would be a real foot-gun — every 'except Exception:' clause in a file that imported the enum would behave unexpectedly. Catch reads acceptably (Catch.TRY_STATEMENT, Catch.RESCUE) and doesn't pollute exception-handling namespaces.

decisions.chose: Keep Operator as its own enum despite only 3 members.
decisions.rationale: Operators are expression-level constructs; conditionals and loops are statement-level. Folding Operator into Conditional would mix categories. The small enum documents intent and has room to grow if future metrics inspect other operator-bearing nodes.

decisions.chose: Place declarator-chain nodes (function_declarator, pointer_declarator, …) in Wrapper.
decisions.rationale: They wrap the identifier in C-family function definitions, just as template_declaration wraps the function definition. 'Wrapper' generalises 'things the walker descends through' across C/C++ declarator chain, C++ templates, and Julia signature.

decisions.chose: Re-export all 9 enums from slop.language.ast/__init__.py for one-line imports.
decisions.rationale: Grammar files use 6-8 of the 9 enums each; importing from individual modules would mean 6-8 import lines per grammar. The re-export keeps to a single import line per grammar (e.g. 'from ..ast import Callable, Catch, Conditional, Loop, Operator, Scope, Switch').

decisions.chose: Alias Callable as Node in C/C++/Ruby/Julia grammars (where extract_name references Callable members).
decisions.rationale: Avoids the 'Callable' identifier reading ambiguously when typing.Callable might be expected in module scope. 'Node' inside extract_name reads as 'a tree-sitter node type', narrower than 'Callable'. The other grammars (Python/JS/TS/Go/Java/C#/Rust) don't have extract_name overrides and reference Callable only at class-body scope where the alias isn't needed.

review_notes: 486 tests pass — identical to pre-split count. Pure organizational refactor.
The flat Node enum landed in commit ef0e8a8 (one commit ago); this commit (fd50672) is the user-requested follow-up to split it semantically. No flat Node anywhere in the codebase now.

Name collisions between slop.language.ast.{Callable,Scope} and slop.tree.records.{Callable,Scope} are intentional. Different packages, different roles; files using both alias one (the grammar files that need both alias Callable as Node).

architectural_claims: slop.language.ast holds 9 per-category StrEnums, one per module. Each enum is a flat list of tree-sitter type strings in that semantic category. Grammars import only the categories they reference.
Naming convention: enum class name is the semantic category (Conditional, Loop, Switch); member name is the uppercase tree-sitter type string verbatim (IF_STATEMENT, FOR_STATEMENT, SWITCH_CASE).
migration_notes: Any code referencing the flat Node enum from the prior commit needs to switch to the per-category enums. The flat Node lived for ~1 commit on dev; no external API consumers expected.
